post.summary: Publish a custom event from game server
Issue SSE ticket for plugin events

get.summary: List backend instances visible to plugin

post.summary: Report player join from game server
Report player leave from game server
Publish a custom event from proxy
Issue SSE ticket for proxy events

get.summary: List backend instances visible to proxy

get.description: Returns all join tokens.

post.description: Create a join token for a node. Returns 409 if the node already exists, is currently connected, or has a pending token.

409.description: Node already exists, connected, or has pending token

delete.description: Delete/revoke a join token by ID.

get.description: Returns paginated audit log entries. `page` and `pageSize` are the canonical pagination parameters; legacy `offset` and `limit` aliases remain accepted for compatibility.

post.description: Change the authenticated user's password. New password must be at least 8 characters.
Authenticate with username and password. Returns a JWT token and the authenticated user.
Revoke the bearer token used on this request by adding its JTI to the Redis-backed JWT revocation store until the token's natural expiry. Returns 501 when the controller has no Redis configured.

501.description: JWT revocation requires Redis to be configured

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile.

post.description: Public, unauthenticated endpoint that consumes a reset token and sets a new password. Returns 400 for invalid/expired tokens or weak passwords. Returns 404 when password reset is disabled.

400.description: Invalid token, expired token, or weak password

404.description: Password reset not enabled on this controller

post.description: Public, unauthenticated endpoint that initiates a password reset for the given email. Always returns 202 Accepted regardless of whether the email exists, to prevent account enumeration. Returns 404 when password reset is disabled (`security.passwordReset.enabled=false`).

202.description: Reset request accepted (email may or may not exist)

404.description: Password reset not enabled on this controller

post.description: Exchange a valid JWT for a new token.

get.description: Returns all catalog entries across all platforms.

post.description: Adds a new version to the catalog for the specified platform.

patch.description: Updates metadata for an existing catalog version.

delete.description: Removes a version from the catalog.

put.description: Marks the specified version as the recommended version for the platform.

get.description: Returns crash report summaries with optional filtering by group and node. Pagination prefers `page` and `pageSize`; `limit` remains as a deprecated compatibility alias for `pageSize`.
Returns crash counts bucketed over the requested window for sparkline rendering. Default window is 24h with 24 hourly buckets. Window is clamped to the crash retention TTL (30 days).

parameters.name: buckets
parameters.description: Number of buckets to divide the window into (1-288).

get.description: Returns a single crash report including the log tail.

post.summary: Share a crash report via pste
post.description: Uploads the crash detail (including redacted log tail) to the configured paste service and returns the resulting link.

get.description: Returns all entries configured under `controller.yaml: events:`. Entries are configuration-only in v1 and are not API-mutated; admins edit the YAML and restart the controller.

Returns the overlays whose firing window covers the current instant. At most one overlay is active per group; when several entries cover the same group, the one whose firing started most recently wins.

Server-Sent Events stream. Authenticate via the `ticket` query parameter obtained from the ticket endpoint. Sends a `connected` event on initial connection. Events include a `type` field matching CloudEvent types. Max 100 concurrent clients.

post.summary: Issue SSE ticket for events stream
post.description: Exchange a JWT for a short-lived SSE ticket used to authenticate the event stream connection.

get.description: Returns every configured group with computed running instance and player counts.

post.description: Creates a new group. The name is validated via InputValidator.requireSafeName (alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores, 1-64 chars).

get.description: Returns a single group by name.

patch.description: Merges the supplied fields into the existing group configuration. Only fields present in the request body are updated; omitted fields are left unchanged.

get.description: Returns the group with all inherited fields resolved from the parent chain.

post.description: Triggers a rolling restart of all instances in the group.
Schedules one or more new instances for the group.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Number of instances to schedule (1-50). Deprecated: send `count` in the request body instead.

get.description: Returns the current maintenance mode configuration.
Returns a summary of key metrics.

get.summary: Resolve extension variants for a runtime
Browse modules offered by the configured registries
get.description: Aggregates every configured module registry's index. Optional ?q= filters by case-insensitive substring of moduleId or tags.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Substring filter over moduleId and tags

post.summary: Install a module pulled from a configured registry
post.description: Resolves {moduleId, version?} against the configured registries, downloads the signed JAR, verifies its sha256 against the index and its signature against the controller trust root, then installs it. Optional registryUrl must be one of the configured registries.

404.description: Module/version not found in any registry

409.description: No registries configured or install conflict

post.summary: Install a new platform module from JAR
Hot-reload a module's frontend bundle without touching its classloader
post.description: Accepts a zipped frontend bundle (same structure as META-INF/frontend/ inside a module jar) and re-stages it on disk. Publishes MODULE_FRONTEND_RELOADED so the dashboard re-imports the bundle. The platform module's REST routes, capabilities, and module-data stay live — only the dashboard-facing assets are swapped. Used by `prexorctl module dev` for fast frontend iteration.

200.description: Frontend reloaded; body carries the new contentHash

get.summary: Latest self-reported health of a platform module

get.description: Public static asset (no auth) — module asset paths are content-hash addressed and loaded via <script>/<link> tags that cannot send Authorization headers.
Returns all configured network compositions.

post.description: Creates a new network composition. The name is validated via InputValidator.requireSafeName and must match [a-z0-9_][a-z0-9_-]*. All referenced groups (lobbyGroup, fallbackGroups, memberGroups, proxyGroups) must already exist; proxyGroups entries must reference proxy-platform groups.

put.description: Replaces the network composition. The body name must match the path. Cross-reference validation against existing groups is re-applied.

get.description: Returns connected, registered-disconnected, and pending (active join token) nodes.

post.summary: Cordon a node (no new schedules, existing instances stay)

parameters.name: shutdown
parameters.description: Whether the node shuts down after drain. Default true.

parameters.name: kickMessage
parameters.description: Message shown when players are kicked.

get.description: Returns recent records from the per-node daemon log ring buffer.

parameters.name: level
parameters.description: Minimum level (TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR). Defaults to INFO.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return (clamped to 1000).

post.summary: Share recent daemon logs via pste
post.description: Uploads a filtered, redacted slice of the per-node daemon log ring buffer to the configured paste service.

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream tailing the per-node daemon log ring buffer. Authenticate via the `ticket` query parameter obtained from `/api/v1/nodes/{id}/logs/ticket`.

post.summary: Issue SSE ticket for daemon log stream
post.description: Exchange a JWT for a short-lived SSE ticket used to authenticate the daemon log stream connection.

get.description: Bucketed history of node metrics. Sampled every 15s; rebuilt on controller restart.

post.description: Sends a ShutdownNode command straight to the daemon — no drain. The daemon stops its running instances and exits; the scheduler reschedules them onto other nodes if capacity allows. Use `node drain` instead for a graceful, instance-preserving stop.

get.description: Returns high-level counts for the dashboard. Requires authentication but no specific permission.
Bucketed history for cluster-wide counts (`players`, `instances`, `onlineNodes`). Sampled every 15s in-memory; rebuilt on controller restart. Empty buckets contain `null`.
Returns all currently online players across all instances.
Returns a single player by their UUID. Returns 400 if the UUID format is invalid.

Returns the raw per-player event log (joins, proxy-observed transfers, crashes affecting the player, disconnects) recorded by the controller, newest first. This is an append-only audit log; modules layer typed stage interpretations on top of it in their own storage.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries to return (1..1000, default 100). Ignored when 'since' is provided.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: ISO-8601 instant; returns entries with a timestamp >= this value. Mutually exclusive with 'limit'.

post.description: Queues a player transfer to the specified target instance. Returns 404 if the target instance does not exist.

404.description: Player or target instance not found

get.description: Returns all roles including built-in and custom roles.

post.description: Create a custom role. Name must match `[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*` and be at most 32 characters.

get.description: Returns a single role by its uppercase name.

patch.description: Update the permissions of a custom role. ADMIN role permissions are immutable.

delete.description: Delete a custom role. Built-in roles cannot be deleted. Roles with assigned users cannot be deleted (409).

409.description: Role has assigned users; reassign them first

get.description: Returns all instances, optionally filtered by group, state, or node.

delete.description: Removes a STOPPED, CRASHED, or SCHEDULED instance.

409.description: Instance not in a deletable state

post.description: Sends an arbitrary console command to the instance.

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream of stdout/stderr lines from the instance's managed process. Authenticate via the `ticket` query parameter from `/api/v1/events/ticket`. Replays buffered history on connect.
Returns console history for the instance, optionally filtered by `since`/`until` (ISO-8601).

post.summary: Share instance console history via pste
post.description: Uploads the recent console history of the instance to the configured paste service after server-side redaction. Limit defaults to 500 lines (clamped to 5000).

get.summary: Read a bounded slice of a single file under an instance directory
get.description: Returns up to `maxBytes` of a single file (head by default; `tail=true` for the last bytes). Path-traversal is rejected daemon-side; symlinks are never followed.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Relative path under the instance working directory

parameters.name: maxBytes
parameters.description: Cap on bytes returned (default 64 KiB, ceiling 1 MiB)

parameters.name: tail
parameters.description: Return the LAST maxBytes instead of the first

get.description: Bucketed history of instance metrics (`tps1m`, `msptAvg`, `heapUsedMb`, `playerCount`). Sampled every 15s; rebuilt on controller restart. Empty buckets contain `null`.
Returns proxy-level metrics for the instance.
Returns recent share records (paginated, newest first). Filter by `kind` and `activeOnly`.

parameters.name: kind
parameters.description: Filter by surface (CRASH, CONTROLLER_LOGS, DAEMON_LOGS, DIAGNOSTICS, INSTANCE_CONSOLE)

get.summary: Get a paste share by id

post.description: Calls pste DELETE using the stored delete token, marks the local record revoked, and audits.

get.description: Pulls together version, readiness, settings, redacted config, redis keyspace summary, lease snapshot, and cluster overview behind a single ADMIN-gated read so `prexorctl diagnostics bundle` doesn't fan out separate calls.

post.summary: Share the diagnostics bundle via pste
post.description: Renders the expanded diagnostics bundle (config + cluster overview + nodes/instances/groups/templates/per-instance filetrees) as a redacted text document and uploads it to the configured paste service.

get.description: Returns system health status. Requires authentication but no specific permission.

post.summary: Share recent controller logs via pste
post.description: Uploads a filtered, redacted slice of the controller log ring buffer to the configured paste service.

get.description: Server-Sent Events stream tailing the controller log ring buffer. Authenticate via the `ticket` query parameter obtained from `/api/v1/system/logs/ticket`.

post.summary: Issue SSE ticket for controller log stream

get.description: Returns whether the controller is ready to serve requests.

post.description: Gracefully stops the controller process this request is served by. Responds 202 first, then exits shortly after so the response can flush. In an HA cluster this stops only the targeted controller; the remaining peers re-elect a leader. ADMIN-only.
Multipart form: name, description, platform fields plus a tar.gz file field.

post.summary: Extract a ZIP already in the template

parameters.name: delete
parameters.description: Delete the source ZIP after extraction

post.description: Multipart form. ZIP archives may be auto-extracted with `extract=true`.

422.description: No snapshot for the requested hash

patch.description: Self-update: users can change their own username. Changing role or password of others requires users.update permission.

post.description: Multipart form upload. Accepts PNG/JPG/GIF/WebP up to 5 MB.

get.description: Returns all currently issued workload (plugin/proxy) credentials. Each credential is bound to a specific instance and authenticates that instance against `/api/plugin/*` and `/api/proxy/*`.

delete.summary: Revoke all workload credentials for an instance
delete.description: Revokes every workload credential currently bound to the given instance. Returns 404 when no credentials are found for the instance.

404.description: No workload credentials found for this instance

delete.description: Revokes a single workload credential by its token ID.
